publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 156. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: I think you will wish to have a line. I have had a long & painful process performed on my teeth this morning, & have to go again now. A sad business, & shockingly painful.

I am thus detained till the 5 Train; & can't let the carriage free for Dr G.-- & B-- till that hour. So they won't be down till 8 or 9 o'clock. Byron is very unwell with a sick headache, poor thing.--
I shall write to you more at full from Brighton tomorrow

but I want now just to express to you my deep sense of the degree in which you (dearest Mate) depend on me, on my diligence, & discretion; & how entirely I perceive that your strong attachment to & feelings for me cause you to be at my mercy; I am more aware of this than I was, & I hope I am incapable of abusing such a fact.--
Of course time only can enable you to judge how far I am capable of repaying your own devotion of feeling.
I merely wish you to be aware that I feel my reponsibility on the subject very deeply, & that I reflect much & continually on it now.
